sets.passage: For centuries, reading meant the silent, sustained engagement of a single mind with a printed page, an activity that demanded patience and rewarded it. That experience is now being (1)____ by screens that fragment attention into ever smaller units. Scrolling, notifications, and hyperlinks (2)____ the reader away from any extended argument, rewarding speed over depth and novelty over reflection. Cognitive scientists warn that this shift may be quietly (3)____ our capacity for the slow, immersive reading on which complex thought once depended. Few people today can (4)____ a demanding essay without reaching for a more stimulating tab, and even devoted readers notice their concentration fraying. Yet the picture is not uniformly bleak. Digital tools also make an (5)____ amount of text available to anyone with a connection, dissolving barriers that once kept knowledge locked in distant libraries and private collections. Search engines let curious minds (6)____ a topic in minutes rather than weeks. The real danger, some scholars (7)____, is not the disappearance of reading altogether but the steady narrowing of its purpose to instant retrieval. To preserve deep literacy, educators are experimenting with (8)____ that deliberately slow students down, asking them to annotate, reread, and discuss what they encounter. Such practices treat attention as a skill to be (9)____ rather than a finite resource to be spent and exhausted. Whether the next generation will inherit both the speed of the screen and the patience of the page remains (10)____. The future of reading, in short, will be decided less by technology than by the habits we choose to cultivate around it.
sets.explain_zh: (1)displaced 被动语态填空，指阅读体验被屏幕“取代”，搭配 being+过去分词。(2)distract 动词原形作复数主语 Scrolling, notifications, and hyperlinks 的限定谓语，distract the reader away from 把读者从延展论证中“分散”注意力（须用原形 distract 而非分词）。(3)eroding 现在分词，be quietly eroding our capacity 缓慢“侵蚀”能力。(4)tackle 动词原形，跟在情态动词 can 之后，tackle a demanding essay 应对一篇高难文章。(5)enormous 形容词修饰 amount，an enormous amount of text 巨量文本。(6)explore 动词原形，let minds explore a topic 探究一个主题。(7)contend 动词，some scholars contend 学者们“主张/论辩”，后接 that 从句。(8)techniques 名词复数，experimenting with techniques 试验各种方法。(9)cultivated 过去分词，a skill to be cultivated 一项需要“培养”的技能。(10)uncertain 形容词作 remains 的表语，仍“不确定”。干扰项：consuming 虽与 reading 相关但语义/位置均不符（其作定语义为“消耗性的”，无被动或搭配位置）；shifting 现在分词义偏“自身转移”而非取代他者；abundant 与 amount 搭配不自然（数量本身不“丰沛”），故失败；fragrant 形容词义为“芳香的”，仅修饰气味，置于任何空格（如 a fragrant amount of text）均语义荒谬，作纯干扰；device 名词单数，与 techniques 复数槽位及“实验方法”语义不合。

sets.passage: Replacing fossil fuels with clean power is often described as a simple matter of building enough wind turbines and solar panels and waiting for the old plants to close. The reality is far more (1)____. Renewable output fluctuates constantly with the weather, so grids must somehow (2)____ supply with demand at every instant, second by second. Without large-scale storage, surplus electricity generated cheaply at noon is simply (3)____ when the sun sets and household consumption peaks. Batteries help, but their materials are expensive and their extraction carries an environmental (4)____ of its own, from scarred landscapes to polluted rivers. A second obstacle is the aging infrastructure that was designed for a handful of large centralized plants, not for millions of (5)____ rooftop generators feeding power back into the system in unpredictable bursts. Upgrading these networks demands enormous capital and decades of patient (6)____ that few governments are eager to fund. Politically, the transition also threatens entire regions whose economies still (7)____ on coal or oil, raising fears of unemployment that opponents are quick to (8)____ for their own ends. Engineers therefore argue that the shift must be (9)____ rather than abrupt, pairing renewables with efficiency measures and, for the time being, some conventional backup capacity. Critics counter that gradualism is merely a convenient excuse for endless (10)____, and that the climate simply cannot wait for comfortable political timetables. Between these competing positions lies the difficult, unglamorous, and largely invisible work of building an entirely new system that is at once cleaner, cheaper, and reliable enough to earn and keep the lasting trust of an anxious and skeptical public.
sets.explain_zh: (1)complicated 形容词作表语，far more complicated 远比想象复杂。(2)balance 动词原形跟在 must 后，balance supply with demand 平衡供需。(3)wasted 过去分词作表语，surplus is wasted 盈余被“浪费”。(4)cost 名词，environmental cost 环境代价（carries a cost of its own）。(5)scattered 形容词，scattered rooftop generators 分散的屋顶发电源。(6)investment 名词，patient investment 耐心的投资。(7)depend 动词，economies depend on coal，固定搭配 depend on。(8)exploit 动词原形，opponents exploit fears 反对者“利用”恐惧。(9)gradual 形容词，be gradual rather than abrupt 渐进而非突然。(10)delay 名词，excuse for delay 拖延的借口。干扰项：velvet 名词“天鹅绒”，作物质名词无法占据任何动词/形容词槽（must somehow velvet supply 荒谬），作纯干扰；expense 与 cost 近义，但 carry an expense 不地道、environmental expense 不搭，故失败；marble 名词“大理石”，物质名词，economies still marble on coal 语义荒谬，无法占据 (7) 的动词槽，作纯干扰；spread 作形/分词义为“扩散”，不修饰 generators 的“分散分布”且无被动结构支撑，故失败。

sets.passage: Fashion is frequently dismissed as something frivolous, yet sociologists treat it as a serious (1)____ of how societies negotiate identity and status. What we wear is rarely a purely personal choice; it is shaped by forces that (2)____ from advertising to peer pressure to deep cultural memory and inherited taste. A single garment can signal wealth, rebellion, or belonging, often all of these at once. The famous theory of conspicuous consumption holds that the affluent adopt costly styles precisely in order to (3)____ themselves from those who cannot possibly afford them. Once a fashion spreads downward and becomes common, the elite quietly (4)____ it and move on to something new, keeping the whole cycle in perpetual motion. This restless (5)____ explains why trends feel at once inevitable and absurd. Yet fashion is not merely imposed from above by designers and the rich. Subcultures repeatedly (6)____ mainstream taste, taking discarded or stigmatized items and (7)____ them with entirely new meaning. Punk safety pins and worn denim began life as gestures of working-class defiance before industry eagerly absorbed and (8)____ them for profit. The sociologist's task is therefore to read these endless movements as a (9)____ language, one in which hemlines, fabrics, colours, and logos all carry subtle messages that ordinary words alone could never hope to convey. Far from being trivial or merely decorative, then, the everyday act of getting dressed each morning is in truth a (10)____ negotiation between the individual and the collective, between the deep human wish to fit in and belong and the equally deep and contradictory wish to stand apart from the crowd as someone distinct.
sets.explain_zh: (1)mirror 名词，a serious mirror of how societies… 把时尚视为反映社会的“镜子”。(2)range 动词，forces that range from…to… 从…到…“涵盖”。(3)distinguish 动词原形（in order to 后），distinguish themselves from 把自己与他人“区分”。(4)abandon 动词，the elite abandon it 精英“抛弃”它。(5)turnover 名词，restless turnover 不停的“更替”。(6)challenge 动词，subcultures challenge mainstream taste 挑战主流品味。(7)investing 现在分词，taking…and investing them with new meaning 赋予新意义。(8)commercialized 过去分词，absorbed and commercialized them 吸收并“商业化”。(9)silent 形容词，a silent language 无声的语言。(10)constant 形容词作定语，a constant negotiation 持续的协商。干扰项：reflect 为动词，(1) 处需名词中心词作 a serious ___ of，reflect 无法占位，故失败；vary 与 range 近义但 vary from…to… 表“个体变动/变化”，不符“各种力量横跨”，故失败；separate 与 distinguish 近义，但 separate themselves from 偏物理“分开”，(3) 强调以风格“相区别”取 distinguish，separate 作干扰；renewal 义为“更新/续约”，缺乏“淘汰旧上新”的轮替义，不及 turnover 贴切，故失败；granite 名词“花岗岩”，物质名词，in order to granite themselves from 语义荒谬，无法占据 (3) 的动词槽，作纯干扰；edible 形容词义为“可食用的”，an edible language 语义荒谬，无法占据 (9) 的形容词槽，作纯干扰。

sets.passage: Before a scientific paper reaches the public, it usually passes through peer review, a process in which independent experts (1)____ the work for flaws and overstatements. In principle, this gatekeeping (2)____ that only sound research enters the published literature. Reviewers carefully check whether the methods are appropriate, whether the data actually (3)____ the conclusions, and whether the claims do not quietly exceed the evidence. When the system works as intended, it (4)____ readers a reasonable measure of confidence that what they are reading has survived informed and skeptical scrutiny. In practice, however, peer review is far from (5)____. Reviewers are typically unpaid, overworked, and sometimes unconsciously biased toward results that (6)____ their own cherished theories. Fraud and honest error occasionally slip through the net anyway, only to be exposed years later, long after the damage to public trust has already been done. Critics also frequently note that the process can be painfully (7)____, delaying important and time-sensitive findings for many months at a stretch. Some journals have recently begun to (8)____ with open review, publishing referee reports alongside the names of those who wrote them, in the hope that transparency will improve overall (9)____. Others experiment instead with publishing first and reviewing the work openly afterward, in full view of the entire research community. None of these well-intentioned reforms, however, truly abolishes the basic underlying (10)____ on the fallible judgment of human experts, which nevertheless remains, for all of its obvious and well-documented imperfections, the least bad method that science has yet managed to devise for the hard task of separating genuine, durable knowledge from confident and well-presented noise.
sets.explain_zh: (1)scrutinize 动词，experts scrutinize the work for flaws 审查找瑕疵。(2)ensures 第三人称单数，this gatekeeping ensures that… 确保。(3)support 动词，the data support the conclusions 数据支持结论。(4)grants 第三人称单数，it grants readers confidence 给予信心。(5)flawless 形容词作表语，far from flawless 远非完美。(6)confirm 动词，results that confirm their own theories 证实自身理论。(7)slow 形容词，painfully slow 缓慢得令人难受。(8)experiment 动词原形（have begun to），experiment with open review 尝试公开评审。(9)accountability 名词，improve accountability 提高问责。(10)reliance 名词，reliance on expert judgment 对专家判断的依赖。干扰项：mosaic 名词“马赛克/镶嵌画”，无法占据 (1) 的动词槽（experts mosaic the work 荒谬），作纯干扰；granite 名词“花岗岩”，gatekeeping granite that 荒谬，无法占据 (2) 的动词槽，作纯干扰；edible 形容词“可食用的”，peer review is far from edible 语义荒谬，无法占据 (5) 槽，作纯干扰；furniture 名词“家具”，underlying furniture on the judgment 荒谬，无法占据 (10) 的名词槽，作纯干扰；uphold 动词义为“维护/支持”，(2)(3)(6) 各槽均不通（uphold that 不成立、uphold conclusions/theories 语义偏“维护制度”而非“支持数据/证实理论”），故全盘失败。

sets.passage: The phrase "digital divide" once referred quite simply to who owned a computer and who did not. Today the gap is far more (1)____, shaped less by the bare presence of devices than by the quality of access and the skills needed to use them well. A teenager with a cheap phone and a generous data plan may still be badly (2)____ if her connection is too slow to load a job application or join a virtual classroom. Researchers therefore now carefully (3)____ between mere connectivity and meaningful participation in digital life. The social consequences of this divide are deeply (4)____ and self-reinforcing. Children without reliable internet steadily fall behind in school; adults without it struggle even to (5)____ for benefits or find work, since so many essential public services have migrated online. The recent pandemic made these inequalities painfully (6)____, as households without adequate bandwidth could only watch opportunities (7)____ away in real time. Governments and charities have since poured resources into (8)____ the gap, laying fibre cable and distributing free laptops to those in need. Yet hardware on its own can never fully solve a stubborn problem so deeply rooted in entrenched poverty and decades of uneven education. Lasting solutions must therefore also (9)____ the confidence and the literacy that let ordinary people turn raw access into genuine advantage. Otherwise, simply expanding the network merely relocates the divide somewhere else instead of genuinely closing it, leaving the most (10)____ communities formally connected in name but quietly and persistently excluded in actual fact from the opportunities that connection was supposed to deliver.
sets.explain_zh: (1)subtle 形容词，more subtle 更微妙（不再只是有无设备）。(2)disadvantaged 过去分词作表语，may still be disadvantaged 仍处于不利地位。(3)distinguish 动词原形，distinguish between A and B 区分两者，后有 between 锁定。(4)unequal 形容词，deeply unequal 极不平等（指后果分布）。(5)apply 动词原形，struggle to apply for benefits 申请福利，后有 for。(6)visible 形容词，painfully visible 痛苦地显现。(7)slip 动词原形，watch opportunities slip away 机会“溜走”。(8)bridging 现在分词/动名词，poured resources into bridging the gap 弥合差距。(9)build 动词原形（must also），build the confidence and literacy 建立信心与素养。(10)vulnerable 形容词，the most vulnerable communities 最脆弱的社群。干扰项：wooden 形容词“木制的/呆板的”，the gap is far more wooden 语义荒谬，无法占据 (1) 槽，作纯干扰；separate 与 distinguish 近义，但 (3) 有 between，distinguish between 为固定搭配，separate between 不地道，故失败；edible 形容词“可食用的”，inequalities painfully edible 荒谬，无法占据 (6) 槽，作纯干扰；granite 名词“花岗岩”，watch opportunities granite away 荒谬，无法占据 (7) 的动词槽，作纯干扰；excluded 过去分词，主题相关但 (2) 取 disadvantaged、(10) 取 vulnerable，且 excluded 的概念已在文末自然出现，故作干扰排除。

sets.passage: As machines grow capable of tasks once thought uniquely human, the long debate over automation has shifted from whether jobs will vanish to how quickly, and exactly which ones. Optimists like to point out that every previous wave of technology eventually (1)____ more work than it ever destroyed. Pessimists reply that the current wave is genuinely different in both its raw speed and its (2)____, reaching deep into white-collar professions that long seemed entirely (3)____. Whatever the eventual outcome, millions of workers will need to (4)____ new skills that match a profoundly transformed labour market. This is far easier said than done. Retraining programs are expensive, and adults with families and mortgages cannot simply (5)____ several years to full-time study. Moreover, the very jobs most exposed to automation are often held by precisely those with the fewest resources to (6)____ themselves for new ones. Without deliberate and generous policy, the gains from automation may (7)____ to a narrow class of owners while the heavy costs fall squarely on the displaced. Some economists therefore strongly (8)____ a system of lifelong learning, funded publicly and woven into the ordinary working week rather than (9)____ onto the unemployed as a grudging afterthought. Others argue instead for simply sharing the productivity dividend directly with everyone, through much shorter working hours or a guaranteed basic income for all. What ultimately unites all of these very different proposals is the shared and sober recognition that technology itself is fundamentally (10)____; whether it eventually liberates us or impoverishes us depends almost entirely on the deliberate political choices that societies choose to make around it.
sets.explain_zh: (1)created 过去式，technology created more work 创造了更多工作。(2)scope 名词，speed and scope 速度与范围。(3)secure 形容词作表语，professions that seemed secure 看似稳固/有保障。(4)acquire 动词原形（need to），acquire skills 习得技能。(5)devote 动词原形（cannot simply），devote years to study 投入数年。(6)prepare 动词原形（resources to + 动词），prepare themselves for new ones 做准备，后有 for。(7)flow 动词原形（may），gains may flow to a narrow class 流向少数人，后有 to。(8)advocate 动词，economists advocate a system 主张一套体系。(9)imposed 过去分词，rather than imposed onto the unemployed 而非强加。(10)neutral 形容词作表语，technology is neutral 技术本身中性。干扰项：velvet 名词“天鹅绒”，wave eventually velvet more work 荒谬，无法占据 (1) 的动词槽，作纯干扰；mosaic 名词“马赛克”，its raw speed and its mosaic 荒谬，无法占据 (2) 的名词槽，作纯干扰；edible 形容词“可食用的”，professions seemed entirely edible 荒谬，无法占据 (3) 槽，作纯干扰；granite 名词“花岗岩”，need to granite new skills 荒谬，无法占据 (4) 的动词槽，作纯干扰；fair 形容词，与 neutral 主题相关但 (10) 语义是“技术本身无善恶取向”取 neutral，fair（公平）语义不切，故失败。

sets.passage: Philosophers have argued about the true nature of happiness for as long as they have argued about anything at all. One ancient tradition holds that happiness is simply a matter of (1)____ pleasure and avoiding pain, a view that sounds perfectly obvious until one notices how quickly most pleasures (2)____. The thrill of a new possession fades within days, and we soon find ourselves chasing the next one, trapped on what psychologists vividly call the hedonic treadmill. A rival tradition, closely associated with Aristotle, instead (3)____ that genuine happiness lies not in fleeting sensation but in living a whole life of virtue and meaning. On this account, a person can be deeply (4)____ even while enduring real hardship, provided that the hardship serves some genuinely worthy end. Modern research lends partial support to both camps. Pleasure clearly (5)____ to overall well-being, but careful studies repeatedly show that beyond a fairly modest income, extra money brings sharply (6)____ returns. What best predicts lasting contentment is less wealth than the (7)____ of close relationships and a durable sense of purpose. Perhaps the deepest puzzle of all is that happiness pursued too (8)____ tends instead to (9)____ us entirely. People who constantly stop to ask themselves whether they are truly happy generally discover that they are not. The wiser and more paradoxical strategy, many thoughtful philosophers eventually conclude, is to aim steadily and wholeheartedly at something worthwhile beyond happiness itself, and then simply to let a quiet contentment arrive on its own as an unexpected (10)____ rather than as a deliberately pursued goal.
sets.explain_zh: (1)maximizing 动名词，a matter of maximizing pleasure 使快乐最大化。(2)fade 动词原形，how quickly pleasures fade 快乐消退。(3)claims（索引12）第三人称单数，this tradition claims that… 主张。(4)fulfilled 过去分词作表语，deeply fulfilled 深感充实。(5)contributes 第三人称单数，pleasure contributes to well-being 有助于幸福，后有 to。(6)diminishing 现在分词作定语，diminishing returns 边际递减回报（固定经济学搭配）。(7)quality 名词，the quality of close relationships 亲密关系的质量。(8)directly 副词，pursued too directly 过于直接地追求。(9)elude 动词原形（tends to），tends to elude us 反而躲开我们。(10)byproduct 名词，arrive as a byproduct 作为副产品到来。干扰项：velvet 名词“天鹅绒”，how quickly most pleasures velvet 荒谬，无法占据 (2) 的动词槽，作纯干扰；granite 名词“花岗岩”，instead granite that genuine happiness 荒谬，无法占据 (3) 的动词槽，作纯干扰；increasing 与 diminishing 反义，(6) 处 increasing returns 与“余钱回报递减”相悖，明确失败；edible 形容词“可食用的”，a person can be deeply edible 荒谬，无法占据 (4) 槽，作纯干扰；furniture 名词“家具”（不可数），arrive as an unexpected furniture 既不合语法又荒谬，无法占据 (10) 槽，作纯干扰。

sets.passage: Cities have always depended on the surrounding countryside to feed them, but the distance between farm and fork has never in history been greater. A typical urban meal now (1)____ ingredients that have travelled many thousands of kilometres, refrigerated, processed, and repackaged at every stage of the journey. This long global supply chain is wonderfully efficient in price but dangerously (2)____ in resilience: a single disruption can leave city shelves empty within a matter of days. Increasingly concerned by this (3)____, urban planners are now seriously rethinking how cities actually obtain their food. One promising response is to bring production much closer, (4)____ rooftops, vacant lots, and even disused warehouses into gardens and vertical farms. Such projects rarely (5)____ a large city's full needs, but they meaningfully shorten supply lines, cut waste, and gently reconnect ordinary residents with the (6)____ of what they eat each day. They also turn long-neglected spaces into something genuinely (7)____. Critics rightly caution against romanticism, however: urban agriculture is highly labour-intensive and simply cannot (8)____ the vast economies of scale that conventional rural farming enjoys. A more realistic and promising path may instead (9)____ in redesigning the whole system, from regional food hubs to policies that reward farmers for selling locally. The real and achievable goal is not to make every single city wholly self-sufficient overnight, an obviously impossible (10)____, but rather to weave local food production patiently back into the fabric of urban life, so that the vast systems quietly feeding millions of people each day become less wasteful, less fragile, and far more visible to the ordinary citizens who depend on them.
sets.explain_zh: (1)contains 第三人称单数，a meal contains ingredients 含有原料。(2)fragile 形容词作表语，efficient in price but fragile in resilience 价格高效却脆弱。(3)vulnerability 名词，concerned by this vulnerability 对此脆弱性的担忧。(4)converting 现在分词，converting rooftops…into gardens 把屋顶等改造成花园，后有 into。(5)satisfy 动词原形（rarely + 动词），satisfy a city's needs 满足需求。(6)origins 名词复数，the origins of what they eat 食物来源。(7)productive 形容词，turn spaces into something productive 有产出的东西。(8)match 动词原形（cannot），match the economies of scale 比得上规模经济。(9)lie 动词原形（may），a path may lie in redesigning 出路在于重新设计，后有 in。(10)ambition 名词，an impossible ambition 不可能的雄心。干扰项：mosaic 名词“马赛克”，meal now mosaic ingredients 荒谬，无法占据 (1) 的动词槽，作纯干扰；granite 名词“花岗岩”，dangerously granite in resilience 荒谬，无法占据 (2) 的形容词槽，作纯干扰；velvet 名词“天鹅绒”，concerned by this velvet 语义荒谬（与 resilience 缺陷无关），无法占据 (3) 的语义槽，作纯干扰；furniture 名词“家具”，非 -ing 形式，无法占据 (4) 中 into 前的分词槽（furniture rooftops into gardens 荒谬），作纯干扰；marble 名词“大理石”，rarely marble a city's needs 荒谬，无法占据 (5) 的动词槽，作纯干扰。

sets.passage: Every single time we search, shop, or idly scroll, we leave behind a long trail of data that companies are extremely eager to collect. Taken individually, each fragment seems entirely (1)____; combined and cross-referenced, however, the same fragments can reveal our health, our politics, and our relationships with startling precision. This is precisely why privacy in the data age can no longer be sensibly understood as the simple right to keep secrets. It is far better (2)____ as control over how information about us is gathered, shared, and ultimately used. The real trouble is that such control is, in practice, largely (3)____. Almost no one actually reads the lengthy agreements they routinely (4)____ before installing an app, and fewer still could plausibly refuse without losing access to services that modern life increasingly (5)____. Consent, in other words, has quietly become a polite (6)____. Companies insist that data collection (7)____ better products and genuinely personalized convenience, and to a real degree this is perfectly true. Yet the very same techniques that helpfully recommend a film can also be used to (8)____ a wavering voter or quietly inflate an insurance premium. Regulators everywhere are scrambling to (9)____ rules that protect ordinary citizens without strangling useful innovation, but technology stubbornly evolves faster than law. Ultimately, the thoughtful defenders of privacy argue, the real question facing us is not whether any of us happens to have something to hide, but whether we truly and collectively wish to live in a society where every single action is permanently recorded, endlessly stored, and silently (10)____ by distant commercial parties whom we can never see and never hold to account.
sets.explain_zh: (1)harmless 形容词作表语，each fragment seems harmless 看似无害。(2)conceived 过去分词，better conceived as control 更应被理解为…（be conceived as 固定搭配）。(3)illusory 形容词作表语，such control is largely illusory 这种控制大多虚幻。(4)accept 动词原形，agreements they accept 他们接受的协议。(5)demands 第三人称单数，services that life increasingly demands 生活日益离不开的服务。(6)fiction 名词，consent has become a fiction 同意已成虚构。(7)drives（索引14）第三人称单数，data collection drives better products 推动更好产品。(8)manipulate 动词原形（can also be used to），manipulate a voter 操纵选民。(9)design 动词原形（scrambling to），design rules 制定规则。(10)judged 过去分词，silently judged by parties 被悄悄评判。干扰项：mosaic 名词“马赛克”，each fragment seems entirely mosaic 荒谬，无法占据 (1) 的形容词槽，作纯干扰；imagined 与 conceived 形似，但 be imagined as 偏“凭空想象”，固定表达为 be conceived as，故失败；velvet 名词“天鹅绒”，such control is largely velvet 荒谬，无法占据 (3) 的形容词槽，作纯干扰；require 与 demands 近义，但主语 life 为第三人称单数需 demands，require 为原形形式不符，明确失败；granite 名词“花岗岩”，data collection granite better products 荒谬，无法占据 (7) 的动词槽，作纯干扰。

sets.passage: Creativity is often imagined as the very opposite of economics, a pure realm of inspiration entirely untouched by anything as crude as money. In reality the two are deeply (1)____. Artists, designers, and writers must somehow earn an actual living, and the material conditions under which they work powerfully (2)____ what they ultimately produce. The first great peculiarity of creative markets is that demand is almost impossible to (3)____ in advance. No one can reliably say beforehand which novel will sell millions of copies or which song will come to (4)____ a whole generation, which is exactly why studios and publishers cautiously spread their bets across many projects, fully expecting most of them to fail. A second peculiarity is that the cost of making the very first copy is enormous, while the cost of (5)____ it afterward is now effectively zero. This strange economics of (6)____ explains both the staggering wealth of a few global stars and the chronic precariousness of nearly everyone else. Digital platforms loudly promised to (7)____ ordinary creators by removing the old gatekeepers, yet in practice they have often instead (8)____ rewards toward whoever already commands public attention. Meanwhile, the sheer abundance of free content steadily (9)____ relentless downward pressure on prices, quietly forcing many genuinely talented people to treat their art as a mere sideline. Understanding these hard underlying dynamics truly matters in the end, because any society that genuinely wants a rich, varied, and surprising culture must find practical and reliable ways to actually (10)____ the working creators who supply it day after day, not merely to praise and celebrate them in the abstract while leaving them to struggle.
sets.explain_zh: (1)intertwined 过去分词作表语，deeply intertwined 紧密交织。(2)shape 动词原形，conditions shape what they produce 塑造作品。(3)predict 动词原形（impossible to），demand is impossible to predict 需求难以预测。(4)define 动词原形（will come to），define a generation 定义一代人。(5)reproducing 动名词，the cost of reproducing it 复制它的成本。(6)abundance 名词，economics of abundance 丰裕经济学。(7)empower 动词原形（promised to），empower creators 赋能创作者。(8)concentrated 过去分词，have concentrated rewards toward 把回报集中。(9)exerts 第三人称单数，abundance exerts downward pressure 施加下行压力（exert pressure 固定搭配）。(10)sustain 动词原形（ways to），sustain the creators 供养创作者。干扰项：mosaic 名词“马赛克”，the two are deeply mosaic 荒谬，无法占据 (1) 的形容词/分词槽，作纯干扰；granite 名词“花岗岩”，conditions powerfully granite what they produce 荒谬，无法占据 (2) 的动词槽，作纯干扰；velvet 名词“天鹅绒”，demand is impossible to velvet 荒谬，无法占据 (3) 的动词槽，作纯干扰；furniture 名词“家具”，will come to furniture a whole generation 荒谬，无法占据 (4) 的动词槽，作纯干扰；marble 名词“大理石”，abundance steadily marble relentless pressure 荒谬，无法占据 (9) 的动词槽，作纯干扰。
